title: Diagram suggestions and diagram syntax validation on create/update/comment
description:
Diagrams (Mermaid, PlantUML, D2) render visually in the Kanbus console UI and are a powerful communication tool. Two separate signals address diagrams:

1. Suggestion: when a description or comment does not contain any diagram code block, suggest that one might be appropriate.
2. Error: when a description or comment DOES contain a diagram code block, validate its syntax before accepting it. This already exists for some diagram types (content_validation.rs) but needs to be unified and confirmed to apply on all create/update/comment paths.

Scenario: Suggestion emitted when no diagram is present in a description

Given an agent creates an issue with a description that contains no fenced code block with a diagram language (mermaid, plantuml, d2)
When the CLI creates the issue
Then a suggestion is printed to stderr noting that diagrams can be embedded using fenced code blocks
And the suggestion is a SUGGESTION-level message (non-blocking)
And the suggestion names the supported diagram languages

Scenario: Suggestion emitted when no diagram is present in a comment

Given an agent adds a comment with no diagram code block
When the CLI adds the comment
Then a suggestion is printed to stderr about diagram support

Scenario: No diagram suggestion when a diagram is already present

Given a description or comment contains a fenced code block with language mermaid, plantuml, or d2
When the CLI processes it
Then no diagram suggestion is emitted

Scenario: Broken Mermaid diagram syntax is rejected with an error

Given an agent creates an issue with a description containing a fenced mermaid code block
And the mermaid syntax in that block is invalid
When the CLI validates the content
Then the issue is NOT created
And an error is printed to stderr identifying the broken diagram
And the error message includes the diagram language and the validation failure reason

Scenario: Broken diagram syntax in a comment is rejected

Given an agent adds a comment containing an invalid diagram code block
When the CLI processes the comment
Then the comment is NOT saved
And an error is returned identifying the diagram language and failure

Scenario: Valid diagram passes through without error

Given a description contains a valid mermaid flowchart
When the CLI creates the issue
Then the issue is created with no diagram-related error
And no diagram suggestion is emitted

Scenario: --no-validate bypasses diagram validation but not the suggestion

Given an agent uses --no-validate on a comment containing a broken diagram
Then the diagram syntax error is suppressed
But the suggestion about diagram support is still emitted if applicable (suggestions are never suppressed by --no-validate)
creator: ryan.porter
